data.description:
PLACEHOLDER / FOLLOW-ON EPIC. Scoped OUT of the LLM-workflow engine epic. The
deterministic gate is an *example use* of the workflow engine; making that gate
**trustworthy** (cryptographically defensible "this passed review" claims) is the
concern captured here. Do not start until the workflow-engine epic is underway.

## Context
The LLM-workflow engine (separate epic) lets workflows mix scripted + agentic steps. A
recurring *example* shape is: a deterministic step gathers inputs → an agent step emits
findings → a deterministic gate decides pass/fail → a deterministic step records a signed
validation / protected tag on PASS. The engine only needs to *support* such a workflow.
This epic owns the hard part: making the gate's verdict a **trustworthy, tamper-evident,
non-repudiable attestation** that an LLM cannot forge or influence — and the new store
surfaces (protected tags, commit binding) that a trustworthy gate needs.

Driving use cases (the engine demonstrates these; this epic hardens them):
- code-review workflow over a ticket's attached commits → signed validation on PASS;
- review of an epic → a reserved/protected tag on PASS;
- completion-verification of an epic against its success criteria → signed validation on PASS.

## Research findings (captured 2026-06; preserve — this is the value of the placeholder)

### Trust boundary — "the LLM must never sign its own homework"
- The reviewing agent holds the full "lethal trifecta" (untrusted input = ticket/repo
  content; private access = filesystem + tickets; state-change = could write results) —
  OWASP LLM06 *Excessive Agency*. Prompt injection is unsolved; probabilistic guardrails
  are not a control.
- Required architecture: the agent emits **findings only**; a **deterministic gate** decides
  pass/fail; the **signing key is reachable only by deterministic code**, never by any tool
  granted to an agent step. This must be *demonstrated* (a standing test auditing the
  workflow's tool list + key-path reachability), not asserted.
- Self-preference bias (LLMs rate their own outputs higher) means an agent's output must not
  be its own reference. Defense is structural, not prompt-level.

### The signature must bind to the code + inputs it validates
- VERIFIED in rebar today: `sign_manifest` HMACs `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and
  **deliberately excludes `head_sha`** from the signed bytes (signing.py) — so a signature
  "stays certifiable as the repo evolves." That is correct for attesting *steps*, but WRONG
  for a claim about a *moving* repo: a sign at SHA A stays "certified" after code moves to B.
- VERIFIED: the close-gate freshness check (txn.py:308-320) compares HEAD against the
  **unsigned** `head_sha` record field — attacker-malleable.
- FIX: put `reviewed@<sha>`, `tree@<tree-hash>`, `prompt@<commit>`, `model=<id>`,
  `policy=<ver>` + a `review_result` content hash *inside the manifest* (the manifest IS
  inside the HMAC, so this binds them); the verdict surface HMAC-verifies, extracts the
  signed SHA, and re-checks it against current HEAD. The unsigned `head_sha` becomes
  audit-only. Fail closed on missing/duplicate/malformed tokens; keep the `head_sha=="unknown"`
  guard.
- Bind the snapshot to the SHA: resolve ref→immutable commit (`git rev-parse <ref>^{commit}`)
  FIRST, `git archive` THAT sha, record + verify the tree hash. Handle `.gitattributes
  export-ignore`/submodules (extracted tree can differ from `<sha>^{tree}` → decide hard-error
  vs documented-unreviewed).

### Crypto: ride the standards, don't reinvent
- HMAC is symmetric → any verifier can forge ⇒ no non-repudiation, no public verifiability.
  **Default to Ed25519** for any trustworthy/cross-party gate.
- **Emit the signed result as a standard in-toto / DSSE predicate** so off-the-shelf
  verifiers (`cosign verify-attestation`, `gh attestation verify`) validate it — stop
  shipping a private dialect. Our "manifest of verified steps + verdict" maps onto an in-toto
  Statement (subject = reviewed ref/tree; predicate = verdict + provenance).
- **Sigstore keyless** (Fulcio/Rekor/OIDC) when the verifier is *outside* our trust boundary
  (cross-org): key-management elimination + identity binding + transparency-log audit.
  Overkill for an internal same-trust-domain in-process gate (self-managed Ed25519 suffices).
- **Don't gate what the platform already gates:** CODEOWNERS + required-reviews already give
  "the LLM can't unilaterally authorize" for git merges. The signed gate earns its keep only
  for consequential actions that do NOT flow through a PR (ticket transition, deploy, API call).

### Concurrency / convergence (event-sourced store)
- SIGNATURE is last-writer-wins by UUID tie-break; the store is multi-clone (every write
  auto-pushes to origin/tickets). Key signatures by `(run_id, reviewed-sha)`; the trust
  surface requires a certified signature whose in-manifest sha == current HEAD AND verdict ==
  PASS. With the signed-SHA freshness fix: stale signatures fail freshness; concurrent
  same-SHA PASS signatures are verdict-equivalent (benign); a FAIL produces no signature.

### New store surfaces this epic needs (full event-schema rigor)
- **commits-on-ticket** event (attach commit SHAs as the code-review input). NOTE: this one
  may also be built in the engine epic if an example workflow needs it — coordinate.
- **Protected/reserved tags**: a DISTINCT event type with a processor that refuses/ignores
  conflicting plain-EDIT mutations to the reserved namespace (plain tags are unprotected,
  last-writer-wins). Convergence by HLC-prefix + UUID tie-break (never wall-clock). Exclude
  from reconcile/Jira (session_log precedent). Discoverable rejection (`list-reserved` + a
  specific "X is reserved; only a signed workflow gate may apply it" error). Trust ⇒ Ed25519
  (multi-clone by default).
- Agent input/exfil hardening: secret-glob deny over the snapshot (`git archive` INCLUDES
  committed secrets — `.env`/`*.pem`/`id_*`/`*.key`/`*.p12`/`.npmrc`/`.netrc`/cloud creds),
  applied as a merge over existing `denied_paths`; refs constrained to ancestor-of-a-trusted
  branch; agent-authored comments are executor-stamped provenance, excluded from review-input
  assembly (anti-self-poisoning / anti-stored-injection-via-search) and treated as an egress
  channel (no comment grant on sensitive-ref workflows). We do NOT claim to sanitize other
  untrusted content; defense is structural.

### Prior-art landscape (the pattern is mature; the LLM application is the empty seam)
- Deterministic-gate→signed-attestation is foundation-grade: **in-toto** (CNCF Graduated),
  **Sigstore/Cosign** (OpenSSF Graduated), **SLSA**, **DSSE**, **OPA/Rego** for the gate
  condition; **Danger** for pure-deterministic PR gates.
- Across 27+ surveyed LLM projects, **none** combine (LLM finds) + (deterministic code
  decides) + (attestation outside the model's control). This seam is our novelty — a
  recombination of proven parts.
- Demand to validate before building: audit/compliance + multi-agent trust. If no consumer
  truly needs a portable signed attestation (vs. a platform required-check), this is
  gold-plating — name the user first.

## Success Criteria
- A workflow's gate verdict can be recorded as a **tamper-evident, non-repudiable
  attestation** that binds the reviewed code SHA + inputs and is verifiable by standard
  tooling, with the LLM provably unable to forge or self-authorize it.
- Protected tags exist as a first-class, convergence-safe store surface.
- A named real consumer (audit/compliance or multi-agent trust) justifies the work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A named consumer/requirement is documented (else close as "not needed yet").
- [ ] Threat model written; standing test proves the agent's tool surface cannot reach the
      signing key or the gate decision.
- [ ] Signature binds reviewed SHA + tree + prompt/model/policy inside the signed payload;
      verdict surface re-checks against current HEAD; unsigned `head_sha` demoted to audit.
- [ ] Snapshot is bound to the resolved commit SHA (rev-parse → archive → verify tree).
- [ ] Ed25519 default; signed result emitted as in-toto/DSSE; verifiable by `cosign` /
      `gh attestation verify`; Sigstore-keyless path scoped to cross-org consumers.
- [ ] Protected/reserved tag event type + processor (refuses conflicting plain-EDIT),
      convergence-safe, reconcile-excluded, discoverable rejection.
- [ ] Secret-glob deny + ref constraint + executor-stamped agent-comment provenance/egress.
- [ ] Concurrency: signatures keyed by (run_id, reviewed-sha); distributed double-sign
      reasoned through and tested.
data.title: Deterministic gate security: trustworthy signed validation for LLM workflows (follow-on)
